domain: Traditional Chinese Medicine (TCM) sphygmopalpation / pulse diagnosis
specific_mechanism: TCM pulse diagnosis extracts multi-dimensional clinical state from a single bodily interface (radial artery at cun-guan-chi positions, three pressure depths) by applying a structured discrete taxonomy of ~28 pulse qualities (wiry, slippery, choppy, soggy, hidden, floating, sinking, etc.). Each quality maps to specific organ-system and pathology states. The diagnostic readout is a small structured vocabulary, not a continuous waveform descriptor.
llm_application: Read a single live-cheap LLM probe (one residual-stream layer, top-K logit fingerprint, or attention-pattern summary) as a structured discrete taxonomy of qualitative agent-states rather than as a scalar. Define a small fixed vocabulary of qualities ('wiry'=brittle/over-confident, 'slippery'=evasive, 'soggy'=hallucinating, 'hidden'=refusing, etc.) keyed to operational interventions, and train recognizers per quality. Downstream automation acts on the label, not on a threshold over a scalar.
novelty_claim: Mechanistic interpretability in 2026 uses linear probes on residual streams with semantic categories (e.g., Bloom's Taxonomy in arxiv 2602.17229 — discrete cognitive levels). My candidate proposes a similar discrete-taxonomy-on-probe architecture but specialized to live operational quality-axes for online steering rather than offline cognitive analysis. Distinguishing feature: small fixed clinically-oriented taxonomy + multi-probe-position (cun-guan-chi equivalents at different residual depths) + downstream automation tied to label vocabulary.
